data.title: UniFirst beats bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• UniFirst press release (NYSE:UNF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.31 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $602.22M (+1.9% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased to $68.9 million compared to $64.8 million in the prior year, or 6.3%.
  • The quarterly tax rate decreased to 25.0% compared to 26.2% in the prior year.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and Short-term investments totaled $201.0 million as of March 1, 2025.
  • The Company had no long-term debt outstanding as of March 1, 2025.
  • Cash flow from operating activities was $128.3 million in the first half of fiscal 2025, an increase of 20.2% over the prior year.

  • Financial Outlook

    Mr. Sintros continued, “At this time, we expect our revenues for fiscal 2025 to be between $2.422 billion and $2.432 billion [vs. $2.43B consensus (prior $2.425 billion and $2.440 billion)] which reflects the anticipated negative impact of the Canadian Dollar exchange rate compared to our original expectations. We further expect that our diluted earnings per share will be between $7.30 and $7.70 [vs. $7.17 consensus (prior $6.79 and $7.19)], which reflects improved anticipated operating income in our Core Laundry Operations and an assumption that our Key Initiative costs in fiscal 2025 will approximate $12.0 million, revised down from prior estimates.”

data.title: Leggett & Platt plans $285M sale of aerospace products group
data.description:
Leggett & Platt (NYSE:LEG) reached an agreement to sell its Aerospace Products Group to investment funds managed by Tinicum Inc. in a deal valued at $285 million, the company announced. The sale, which is pending regulatory approval and other closing conditions, is expected to be finalized in 2025, according to an announcement on Wednesday.

After accounting for taxes, the company anticipates net cash proceeds of around $240 million from the transaction.

The Aerospace Products Group designs and manufactures complex tube and duct assemblies primarily for commercial and military aircraft, as well as space launch vehicles. The unit operates seven production facilities in the United States, the United Kingdom and France, and employs approximately 700 people. It generated $190 million in net trade sales in 2024.

The move comes as part of Leggett & Platt’s (NYSE:LEG) ongoing strategic review, which aims to align its portfolio with long-term priorities. The company plans to issue updated full-year guidance for 2025 after the sale is completed and the aerospace unit is excluded from future financial reporting.

data.title: Leggett & Platt sells its Aerospace Products Group for $285M
data.description:
• Leggett & Platt (NYSE:LEG) said on Wednesday that it has signed an agreement to sell its Aerospace Products Group to affiliated funds managed by Tinicum Incorporated for a cash purchase price of $285M.
  • The after-tax cash proceeds are expected to be approximately $240M.
  • The Aerospace Products Group is a supplier of complex, highly engineered tube and duct assemblies for use primarily in commercial and military aircraft platforms and space launch vehicles.
  • The business comprises seven manufacturing facilities located in the U.S., UK, and France and approximately 700 employees with net trade sales of $190M in 2024.
  • This divestiture is part of the ongoing strategic business review, aimed at determining which businesses are the right long-term fit for the company.
  • Leggett & Platt (LEG) shares were up 0.51% in premarket trading.

data.title: Petco rallies after its CEO scoops up more shares
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness Company (NASDAQ:WOOF) rallied in early trading on Wednesday after CEO Joel Anderson picked up more shares in the pet retailer.

In an SEC filing, Petco (NASDAQ:WOOF) disclosed that Anderson purchased 1,586,088 shares in a range of $2.91 to $3.03 for a total amount of more than $4.7 million. Anderson assumed the role of CEO on July 29, 2024, and also serves on Petco's (WOOF) board of directors. Before joining Petco (WOOF), Anderson served as CEO of Five Below (FIVE), where he oversaw significant growth, expanding the company from 366 stores to over 1,500 locations and increasing revenue from $500 million to over $3.5 billion.

With its Q4 earnings report, Petco (WOOF) reported revenue fell 7.3% year-over-year to $1.6 billion, while comparable sales were up 0.5%. Adjusted EBITDA was $96.1 million vs. $105.3 million a year ago. EPS was reported at -$0.05 vs. -$0.02 consensus and -$0.08 a year ago. 

Shares of Petco (WOOF) were up 6.1% in premarket action to $3.14 vs. the 52-week range of $1.41 to $6.29. Short interest on WOOF stands at 11.8% of the total float.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: BlackBerry tops FQ4 estimates; initiates FQ1 and FY guidance below estimates
Greece unveils $27B defense investment plan to support industry
data.description:
Greece on Wednesday announced an ambitious €25 billion ($27 billion) defense investment strategy, set to unfold over the next 12 years. The initiative marks the country’s first long-term military procurement plan and reflects its intent to enhance both national security and its domestic defense sector.

Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis presented the strategy to lawmakers on Wednesday, emphasizing Greece’s strategic significance despite its modest size.

“Greece may be small, but it holds significant geopolitical weight,” he said.

While many European countries are ramping up military spending in response to ongoing conflict in Ukraine and shifting U.S. security commitments, Greece has long prioritized defense. Even during its financial crisis, the country consistently exceeded NATO’s defense spending benchmark of 2% of GDP — a reflection of its tense relationship with neighboring Turkey.

In the past few years, Greece strengthened its armed forces by procuring advanced weapons systems, including 24 Rafale jets and naval frigates from France in addition to as many as 40 F-35 fighter jets from the United States. Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) is the prime contractor on the F-35.

Shift from austerity

Greece’s new 12-year roadmap allocates about 10% of its current economic output toward defense procurement. This plan marks a significant shift from the austerity-driven limitations that constrained military spending during Greece’s debt crisis starting in 2010.

Mitsotakis said the defense minister now has clear financial parameters for the program and must make key decisions on how to allocate resources.

A major focus of the plan is to revitalize Greece’s domestic defense industry, which has remained relatively stagnant despite high military expenditures. Mitsotakis said Greek firms will be involved in all future defense initiatives, aiming to ensure the sector plays a larger role in national security efforts.

The strategy also includes modernization efforts that embrace cutting-edge technologies. Greece aims to integrate advanced tools such as unmanned aerial systems, drone defense systems, artificial intelligence and cybersecurity measures to better prepare for the evolving nature of warfare.

Visit to Israel

Earlier this week, Mitsotakis visited Israel to meet with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and key figures in the country’s defense establishment. Discussions centered around deepening defense ties, with an emphasis on technology and innovation.

Beyond equipment and technology, Greece is also investing in its personnel. Defense Minister Nikolaos Dendias recently announced wage increases of up to 20% for military staff. Greece maintains one of the largest per-capita armed forces among NATO members, with more than 1% of its population serving — well above the levels seen in Turkey, the United States or Germany, according to press reports.

data.title: CME sets all-time quarterly ADV record of 29.8M contracts
data.description:
• CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) on Wednesday reported its Q1 and March 2025 market statistics, with average daily volume reaching a new, all-time quarterly record of 29.8M contracts and the second-highest March ADV on record with 30.8M contracts.
  • In Q1, the company's ADV grew 13% year-over-year, with record volume in interest rate, equity index, agricultural, foreign exchange and cryptocurrency products.
  • CME Group's deeply liquid U.S. Treasury complex hit a quarterly ADV record of 9.2M contracts, and its Henry Hub Natural Gas complex set a quarterly ADV record of 1.1M contracts.
  • March ADV grew 27% year-over-year, with record monthly equity index ADV of 9.7M contracts and double-digit ADV growth in interest rate, energy, agricultural, foreign exchange and cryptocurrency products.

data.title: Uranium developers Aero Energy, Kraken Energy to merge
data.description:
Aero Energy (OTCPK:AAUGF) said Wednesday it agreed to acquire uranium development peer Kraken Energy (OTCQB:UUSAF) for C$0.02754/share, or C$1.64M, a 20% premium to the 15-day volume-weighted average trading price of Kraken shares on the Canadian Securities Exchange.

The companies said the deal combines Aero's (OTCPK:AAUGF) Athabasca Basin uranium assets with Kraken's (OTCQB:UUSAF) U.S. uranium assets, capitalizing on the rising priority of domestic assets in a shifting energy landscape.

The transaction will be effected by way of a plan of arrangement requiring the approval of at least two-thirds of the votes cast by Kraken (OTCQB:UUSAF) shareholders.

data.title: BlackBerry slides amid weak outlook for 2026
data.description:
BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) shares fell 3% in premarket trading on Wednesday after the technology company issued an outlook for fiscal 2026 that fell short of Wall Street's estimates.

Looking to the next fiscal year, the Waterloo, Ontario-based company said it expects revenue to be within a range of $504M to $534M, with the mid-point of $519M well below the $550.6M analysts were expecting. It also expects adjusted earnings per share to be between $0.08 and $0.10, below the $0.10 per share estimate.

BlackBerry's forecast for the upcoming quarter also fell short of expectations, as it expects sales to be between $107M and $115M, below the $128.4M analysts were anticipating. Secure communications revenue is forecast to be between $50M and $54M.

The weaker-than-anticipated guidance comes on the back of better-than-expected fourth-quarter results, which were driven in part by BlackBerry's three segments, QNX, Secure Communications and Licensing, CEO John Giamatteo said. Adjusted earnings per share came in at $0.03, $0.03 better than the analyst estimate. Revenue dipped 18.1% year-over-year to $141.7M, but it was still better than the $132.8M analysts had anticipated. Operating cash flow during the period was $42M.

"BlackBerry closed out this transformational fiscal year with another quarter of strong financial performance from all three divisions: QNX, Secure Communications and Licensing," Giamatteo said in a statement. "We also took a big step forward by completing the sale of the Cylance business to Arctic Wolf, a win-win for all parties, and significantly strengthening our balance sheet in the process."

Giamatteo added that the company now has a "solid" balance sheet that "provides optionality for driving shareholder value."

The company is holding a conference call at 8 a.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch launches a new vodka tea brand called Skimmers
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) launched its new vodka tea RTD product this week, according to industry reports. The Skimmers brand will be sold in various 4 and 8 packs. Flavors will include original, half & half, peach, and lemonade tea. The vodka will be sourced from the company's Cutwater brand.

 

 

The vodka tea product is 4.5% ABV, with 100 calories and 2 grams of sugar. Skimmers will initially be available in 18 states and D.C., with a higher concentration in the Midwest, Rockies, and Florida, according to Evercore ISI analyst Robert Ottenstein. Notably, Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) has plans for further expansion for the brand. 

Ottenstein and his team believe that the Skimmers product will help Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) compete better with Surfside and Boston Beer's (SAM) Sun Cruiser, which were noted to be riding strong hard tea category trends. Sazerac's Firefly Sweet Tea Vodka, Heaven Hill Brands' Deep Eddy Sweet Tea Vodka, High Noon, and Boston Beer's (SAM) Twisted Tea are other competitors in the category.
